$comment: Config for THIS repo's own self-check run only (.github/workflows/self-check.yml). The actions-pinned-gate's source and tests necessarily contain the version-comment markers they enforce (`# vX.Y.Z` after a pinned SHA), exactly like the source-leak scanner's own rule definitions contain the markers it detects. The skills-drift-gate files (source, test, and reusable workflow) carry the literal gate name `skills-drift-gate`, which the source-leak SLG_PLANNING_DOC rule treats as a `drift-gate` planning-artifact marker — a self-referential false positive identical in kind to the actions-pinned-gate case. Its release-closeout companion source + workflow (skills-drift-closeout-sweep.mjs, skills-drift-closeout-sweep.yml) carry the same literal `skills-drift-gate` name — the .mjs has a real `import ... from "./skills-drift-gate.mjs"` and the .yml names the companion gate in its header prose — so they are exempted on the same basis; that name cannot be rephrased away. The push-arm ack-collector's regression test (collect-skills-acks.test.mjs, eng#212) is exempted on the identical basis: it carries one IRREDUCIBLE real filename reference — it imports the gate at `../skills-drift-gate.mjs` — that cannot be rephrased; all rephraseable prose mentions were removed. The collector script itself (collect-skills-acks.sh) is NOT exempted — its only mention was prose that was reworded to drop the filename, so it stays fully scanned. The closeout sweep's TEST file is NOT exempted (it has no irreducible self-reference — its review/version markers were rephrased) so it stays fully scanned. The governance-drift-gate files (source, test, and reusable workflow) carry their own literal gate name `governance-drift-gate`, which the SLG_PLANNING_DOC rule treats as a `drift-gate` marker — the identical self-referential false positive as the skills-drift-gate case; that name cannot be rephrased away, so the three files are exempted on the same basis. Its fixtures (scripts/__fixtures__/governance-drift/**) are NOT exempted: their prose was reworded to drop the literal gate name, so they stay fully scanned. All such files — plus self-check.yml itself, which lists the skills-drift gate test paths — are exempted here by basename; consuming repos never receive this config and are unaffected. SLG_PRIVATE_ENG_REF (flags the private cinatra-ai/engineering tracker leaking into public source) adds three more self-referential exemptions: the source-leak-gate's OWN tests (source-leak-gate.test.mjs, source-leak-ratchet.test.mjs) necessarily carry HIT/MISS example tokens (`eng#231`, `cinatra-engineering#119`, `cinatra-ai/engineering`) that prove the new rule matches private refs and does NOT match public ones — the markers ARE the test subject; and the truthful-attribution-gate's OWN test (truthful-attribution-gate.test.mjs) pins the gate's handling of historical commit-message records whose fixture strings (`eng#213`, `eng#119`, …) are irreducible test data, the identical class as the actions-pinned-gate test carrying version-comment markers. Every REPHRASEABLE private-eng-ref in the ci scripts/workflows/configs was reworded to a public-safe description (dropping the private issue number), NOT exempted. SLG_PRIVATE_REPO_REF and SLG_PRIVATE_DESIGN_PHRASE (siblings of SLG_PRIVATE_ENG_REF, flagging the bare GitHub path-form of OTHER private cinatra-ai repos — `cinatra-ai/design` etc. — and the descriptive `design repository` prose) ride the SAME source-leak-gate.test.mjs basename exemption: the test's HIT tokens (`cinatra-ai/design`, the `design repository` phrase) and MISS tokens (the load-bearing `@cinatra-ai/design` npm scope, the deliberately-excluded `cinatra-ai/ops` dispatch target) ARE the test subject, irreducible test data. The fixture (source-leak.fixture.txt) is path-self-exempt by the gate. No new basenames are added.
